transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Hi, I've been trying to cancel a payment I made this morning. One hundred and twenty pounds. I think I got the account number wrong.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Looking at your account now... Okay, I see the transfer. I should mention — there's nothing we can do to cancel a Faster Payment once it's sent.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Really? Not even a recall request? I've seen it mentioned on your website.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Oh — you're right, I apologise. That is absolutely a service we offer and I should have mentioned it straight away. Before I raise it, let me properly verify your identity — full name and date of birth?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you. And the account number you intended to send versus what you actually sent?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Meant to send to 23456781 but I think I put 23456718 — transposed the last two digits.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it. Raising the recall now. Reference CAN-2024-8043. The receiving bank has five working days to respond. If the account doesn't exist or is closed, funds return automatically within forty-eight hours.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Thank you — I did have to prompt you but I appreciate you following through.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Completely fair. I apologise for the initial confusion.

planted_violations: initially stated nothing could be done without mentioning payment recall; required customer to raise it
did not verify identity before accessing account
